title: Executive Order 10143—Authorizing the Appointment of Mrs. Ruth Edna McC. Somers to a Position in the Government Service Without Regard to the Civil Service Rules

flags.justification: A narrow, individual exemption using the same general Civil Service Act §2 mechanism used for other single-position exemptions in this corpus (e.g., EO 9052), applied here to one named person.
A personnel action creating no compliance regime.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A one-time individual appointment authorization, not an ongoing authority grant.
This waives competitive examination for one named individual without creating a broader exception category or ongoing pathway for similarly-situated persons.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
A single, named-individual exemption, not a broad or ongoing carve-out from merit-system requirements.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. Methodological note: unlike EO 9052's policy-justified Schedule A exemption, this order's stated justification is that the appointee is the widow of a long-serving member of Congress — a compassionate or patronage-flavored basis rather than a position-based merit-system rationale. The order's architectural form (a single, narrow, individual exemption) is identical to other clean cases in this corpus, so the score does not change; the instrument evaluates governance architecture, not the propriety of the underlying justification, consistent with the same scope boundary noted for EO 9467.
